sections.paragraph:
προτρέπω in our lit. only mid. (so Hom.+; the prefix focuses on forward motion) 1 aor. προετρεψάμην; 2 aor. pass. 3 sg. προετράπη (ApcrEzk) to promote a particular course of action, urge (on), encourage, impel, persuade (Soph. et al.) τινά someone w. inf. foll. (Hyperid. 6, 24; UPZ 110, 165 [164 b.c.]; PRyl 77, 48; BGU 164, 17; 450, 15; 2 Macc 11:7; Jos., Ant. 12, 166; Just., D. 106, 1) 1 Cl 34:4. Abs. (Jos., Ant. 5, 171; 7, 262) Ac 18:27.—M-M. Spicq.

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

  1. sg. = singular

ApcrEzk ApcrEzk = Apocryphon Ezechiel—List 2

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

inf. inf. = infinitive

foll. foll. = followed, following

Hyperid Hyperid , IV b.c.—List 5

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

PRyl PRyl = Catalogue of the Greek Papyri in the John Rylands Library—List 4

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 889
